884	{- | Enumeration of available system tool capabilities.
885	
886	Each capability represents a system information tool that can be

889	data SystemToolCapability
890	    = -- | Get current date and time
891	      SystemToolDate
892	    | -- | Get operating system information
893	      SystemToolOperatingSystem
894	    | -- | Get environment variables
895	      SystemToolEnvVars
896	    | -- | Get running user information
897	      SystemToolRunningUser
898	    | -- | Get hostname
899	      SystemToolHostname
900	    | -- | Get working directory
901	      SystemToolWorkingDirectory
902	    | -- | Get process information
903	      SystemToolProcessInfo
904	    | -- | Get system uptime
905	      SystemToolUptime
906	    | -- | Attach files for multi-modal interactions
907	      SystemToolAttachFile
908	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
909	
910	-- | Serialize SystemToolCapability as kebab-case strings.
911	instance ToJSON SystemToolCapability where
912	    toJSON SystemToolDate = Aeson.String "date"
913	    toJSON SystemToolOperatingSystem = Aeson.String "operating-system"
914	    toJSON SystemToolEnvVars = Aeson.String "env-vars"
915	    toJSON SystemToolRunningUser = Aeson.String "running-user"
916	    toJSON SystemToolHostname = Aeson.String "hostname"
917	    toJSON SystemToolWorkingDirectory = Aeson.String "working-directory"
918	    toJSON SystemToolProcessInfo = Aeson.String "process-info"
919	    toJSON SystemToolUptime = Aeson.String "uptime"
920	    toJSON SystemToolAttachFile = Aeson.String "attach-file"
921	
922	-- | Parse SystemToolCapability from kebab-case strings.
923	instance FromJSON SystemToolCapability where
924	    parseJSON = Aeson.withText "SystemToolCapability" $ \txt ->
925	        case txt of
926	            "date" -> return SystemToolDate
927	            "operating-system" -> return SystemToolOperatingSystem
928	            "env-vars" -> return SystemToolEnvVars
929	            "running-user" -> return SystemToolRunningUser
930	            "hostname" -> return SystemToolHostname
931	            "working-directory" -> return SystemToolWorkingDirectory
932	            "process-info" -> return SystemToolProcessInfo
933	            "uptime" -> return SystemToolUptime
934	            "attach-file" -> return SystemToolAttachFile
935	            other -> fail $ "Invalid SystemToolCapability: " ++ Text.unpack other ++ ". Expected one of: date, operating-system, env-vars, running-user, hostname, working-directory, process-info, uptime, attach-file."
936	
937	{- | Configuration for a system toolbox.
938	
939	This describes which system information capabilities should be made
940	available to an agent.
941	
942	Example configuration:
943	
944	@
945	{
946	  "tag": "SystemToolbox",
947	  "contents": {
948	    "name": "system",
949	    "description": "System context tools",
950	    "capabilities": ["date", "hostname", "env-vars"],
951	    "envVarFilter": null,
952	    "lifetime": "conversation",
953	    "activation": "always"
954	  }
955	}
956	@
957	
958	The 'envVarFilter' field allows filtering environment variables by
959	name (case-sensitive substring match). If null, all environment
960	variables are exposed.
961	
962	The 'activation' field controls progressive disclosure (default: 'AlwaysActivated').
963	-}

100	
101	{- | Configuration for a filesystem directory as a bash tool source.
102	
103	Describes a directory containing executable bash tools that follow
104	the standard bash-tool protocol (describe|run).
105	
106	Example configuration:
107	
108	@
109	{
110	  "tag": "FileSystemDirectory",
111	  "contents": {
112	    "path": "./tools",
113	    "basenameFilter": null,
114	    "lifetime": "conversation",
115	    "activation": "always"
116	  }
117	}
118	@
119	
120	The optional 'basenameFilter' field allows filtering tools by their
121	filename. If specified, only executables whose names contain the
122	filter string will be loaded.
123	
124	The 'activation' field controls progressive disclosure (default: 'AlwaysActivated').
125	
126	Note: Relative paths are resolved relative to the execution's current
127	working directory.
128	-}
129	data FileSystemDirectoryDescription
130	    = FileSystemDirectoryDescription
131	    { fsDirRoot :: Maybe FilePath
132	    -- ^ Root path, this field is here to support legacy tools definitions
133	    , fsDirPath :: FilePath
134	    -- ^ Path to the directory containing bash tools
135	    , fsDirBasenameFilter :: Maybe Text
136	    -- ^ Optional filter for tool filenames (e.g., ".sh" to load only .sh files)
137	    , fsDirActivation :: Maybe Activation
138	    -- ^ Optional activation mode (default: AlwaysActivated)
139	    }
140	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
141	
142	-- | Custom JSON options for FileSystemDirectoryDescription
143	fsDirOptions :: Aeson.Options
144	fsDirOptions =
145	    Aeson.defaultOptions
146	        { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = dropPrefix "fsDir"
147	        , Aeson.omitNothingFields = True
148	        }
149	  where
150	    dropPrefix prefix str
151	        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str
152	        | otherwise = str
153	
154	instance ToJSON FileSystemDirectoryDescription where
155	    toJSON = Aeson.genericToJSON fsDirOptions
156	    toEncoding = Aeson.genericToEncoding fsDirOptions
157	
158	instance FromJSON FileSystemDirectoryDescription where
159	    parseJSON = Aeson.genericParseJSON fsDirOptions
160	
161	{- | Configuration for a single tool executable.
162	
163	Describes a single executable file to be loaded as a bash tool.
164	This is useful when you want to add a specific tool without loading
165	an entire directory.
166	
167	Example configuration:
168	
169	@
170	{
171	  "tag": "SingleTool",
172	  "contents": {
173	    "path": "/path/to/my-tool.sh",
174	    "lifetime": "conversation",
175	    "activation": "always"
176	  }
177	}
178	@
179	
180	Note: Relative paths are resolved relative to the execution's current
181	working directory.
182	-}
183	data SingleToolDescription = SingleToolDescription
184	    { singleToolPath :: FilePath
185	    -- ^ Path to the single executable tool
186	    , singleToolActivation :: Maybe Activation
187	    -- ^ Optional activation mode (default: AlwaysActivated)
188	    }
189	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
190	
191	-- | Custom JSON options for SingleToolDescription
192	singleToolOptions :: Aeson.Options
193	singleToolOptions =
194	    Aeson.defaultOptions
195	        { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = dropPrefix "singleTool"
196	        , Aeson.omitNothingFields = True
197	        }
198	  where
199	    dropPrefix prefix str
200	        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str

{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedStrings #-}
{-# LANGUAGE ScopedTypeVariables #-}

{- | Provides a runtime for gathering system information.

This module implements the system toolbox functionality, including:

* System information gathering (date, OS info, environment variables, etc.)
* File attachment capability for multi-modal LLM interactions
* Capability-based access control
* Result formatting for LLM consumption
* Tracing for debugging and monitoring

The toolbox can be configured to expose different system information capabilities,
providing contextual information about the running environment to agents.

Example usage:

@
import System.Agents.Tools.SystemToolbox as System
import System.Agents.Base (SystemToolCapability(..))

main :: IO ()
main = do
    let desc = SystemToolboxDescription
            { systemToolboxName = "system"
            , systemToolboxDescription = "System information"
            , systemToolboxCapabilities = [SystemToolDate, SystemToolHostname]
            , systemToolboxEnvVarFilter = Nothing
            }
    result <- System.initializeToolbox tracer desc
    case result of
        Right toolbox -> do
            -- Execute a query for the date capability
            result <- System.executeQuery toolbox "date"
            case result of
                Right queryResult -> print queryResult
                Left err -> print err
        Left err -> putStrLn $ "Failed to initialize: " ++ err
@
-}
module System.Agents.Tools.SystemToolbox (
    -- * Core types
    Trace (..),
    Toolbox (..),
    ToolDescription (..),
    QueryError (..),
    QueryResult (..),
    AttachFileResult (..),

    -- * Initialization
    initializeToolbox,

    -- * Query execution
    executeQuery,
    executeAttachFile,
    getCapabilityInfo,

    -- * Result formatting
    formatResults,

    -- * Media type detection
    detectMediaType,
    getFileExtension,
) where

import Control.Concurrent (threadDelay)
import Control.Concurrent.Async (race)
import Control.Exception (SomeException, try)
import Data.Aeson (ToJSON (..), Value (..), (.=))
import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
import qualified Data.Aeson.Key as AesonKey
import qualified Data.Aeson.KeyMap as KeyMap
import qualified Data.ByteString.Base64 as B64
import Data.ByteString.Char8 (ByteString)
import qualified Data.ByteString.Char8 as BS
import qualified Data.ByteString.Lazy as LByteString
import Data.List (isInfixOf)
import Data.Maybe (catMaybes)
import Data.Text (Text)
import qualified Data.Text as Text
import qualified Data.Text.Encoding as Text
import Data.Time (NominalDiffTime, diffUTCTime, getCurrentTime)
import qualified Data.Time as Time
import Prod.Tracer (Tracer (..), runTracer)
import System.Directory (doesFileExist, getCurrentDirectory)
import System.Environment (getEnvironment, lookupEnv)
import System.FilePath (takeExtension)
import System.IO.Error (isDoesNotExistError)
import System.Posix.Process (getParentProcessID, getProcessID)
import System.Posix.User (getEffectiveGroupID, getEffectiveUserID, getUserEntryForID, homeDirectory, userName, userShell)
import qualified System.Process as Process

import System.Agents.Base (SystemToolCapability (..), SystemToolboxDescription (..))
import System.Agents.Media.Types (ApplicationType (..), AudioType (..), ImageType (..), MediaType (..), TextSubtype (..), VideoType (..))

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Core Types
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | Trace events for monitoring system toolbox operations.

These events allow tracking of:
* System info requests
* File attachment requests
* Query execution progress
* System info retrieval timing
* Capability errors
-}
data Trace
    = -- | System information was requested for a capability
      SystemInfoRequestedTrace !Text
    | -- | File attachment was requested
      FileAttachRequestedTrace !FilePath
    | -- | File was successfully attached
      FileAttachSuccessTrace !FilePath !MediaType !Int
    | -- | File attachment failed
      FileAttachErrorTrace !FilePath !Text
    | -- | System information was retrieved successfully
      SystemInfoRetrievedTrace !Text !NominalDiffTime
    | -- | Error occurred while retrieving system information
      SystemInfoErrorTrace !Text !Text
    deriving (Show)

{- | Description of a system tool.

Contains metadata about a specific system information tool, including
its name, description, and associated toolbox information.
-}
data ToolDescription = ToolDescription
    { toolDescriptionName :: Text
    -- ^ Name of the tool (e.g., "get_date")
    , toolDescriptionDescription :: Text
    -- ^ Human-readable description of what the tool does
    , toolDescriptionToolboxName :: Text
    -- ^ Name of the toolbox this tool belongs to
    }
    deriving (Show)

{- | Runtime state for a system toolbox.

The toolbox maintains:
* Toolbox name and description
* List of enabled capabilities
* Optional environment variable filter pattern
* The original configuration description used to create this toolbox
-}
data Toolbox = Toolbox
    { toolboxName :: Text
    , toolboxDescription :: Text
    , toolboxCapabilities :: [SystemToolCapability]
    , toolboxEnvVarFilter :: Maybe Text
    , toolboxConfig :: SystemToolboxDescription
    -- ^ Original configuration description used to create this toolbox
    }

{- | Result of a system information query.

Contains:
* Capability name
* Data as JSON value
* Execution time
-}
data QueryResult = QueryResult
    { resultCapability :: Text
    , resultData :: Aeson.Value
    , resultExecutionTime :: NominalDiffTime
    }
    deriving (Show)

-- | JSON serialization for QueryResult.
instance ToJSON QueryResult where
    toJSON result =
        Aeson.object
            [ "capability" .= resultCapability result
            , "data" .= resultData result
            , "executionTime" .= formatExecutionTime (resultExecutionTime result)
            ]

{- | Result of a file attachment operation.

Contains:
* File path
* MIME type
* Base64-encoded file content
* File size in bytes
-}
data AttachFileResult = AttachFileResult
    { attachFilePath :: FilePath
    , attachMimeType :: Text
    , attachBase64Data :: Text
    , attachFileSize :: Int
    }
    deriving (Show)

-- | JSON serialization for AttachFileResult.
instance ToJSON AttachFileResult where
    toJSON result =
        Aeson.object
            [ "path" .= attachFilePath result
            , "mimeType" .= attachMimeType result
            , "base64Data" .= attachBase64Data result
            , "size" .= attachFileSize result
            ]

-- | Errors that can occur during system info queries.
data QueryError
    = -- | The requested capability is not enabled in the toolbox
      CapabilityNotEnabledError !Text
    | -- | Error occurred while gathering system information
      SystemInfoError !Text
    | -- | File not found for attach-file
      FileNotFoundError !FilePath
    | -- | File too large for attachment
      FileTooLargeError !FilePath !Int
    | -- | Unsupported file type
      UnsupportedFileTypeError !FilePath !Text
    deriving (Show, Eq)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Initialization
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | Initialize a system toolbox from a description.

This function creates a 'Toolbox' value from a 'SystemToolboxDescription',
validating the configuration and preparing the runtime state.

Returns an error if the configuration is invalid.
-}
initializeToolbox ::
    Tracer IO Trace ->
    SystemToolboxDescription ->
    IO (Either String Toolbox)
initializeToolbox _tracer desc = do
    -- Validate that we have at least one capability
    if null desc.systemToolboxCapabilities
        then pure $ Left "System toolbox must have at least one capability enabled"
        else do
            let toolbox =
                    Toolbox
                        { toolboxName = desc.systemToolboxName
                        , toolboxDescription = desc.systemToolboxDescription
                        , toolboxCapabilities = desc.systemToolboxCapabilities
                        , toolboxEnvVarFilter = desc.systemToolboxEnvVarFilter
                        , toolboxConfig = desc
                        }
            pure $ Right toolbox

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Media Type Detection
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | Detect media type from file extension.

Returns Nothing if the file type is not recognized as a supported media type.
-}
detectMediaType :: FilePath -> Maybe MediaType
detectMediaType path =
    case Text.toLower $ Text.pack $ takeExtension path of
        -- Images
        ".png" -> Just $ MediaImage ImagePNG
        ".jpg" -> Just $ MediaImage ImageJPEG
        ".jpeg" -> Just $ MediaImage ImageJPEG
        ".gif" -> Just $ MediaImage ImageGIF
        ".webp" -> Just $ MediaImage ImageWebP
        ".svg" -> Just $ MediaImage ImageSVG
        -- Audio
        ".mp3" -> Just $ MediaAudio AudioMP3
        ".wav" -> Just $ MediaAudio AudioWAV
        ".ogg" -> Just $ MediaAudio AudioOGG
        ".aac" -> Just $ MediaAudio AudioAAC
        ".flac" -> Just $ MediaAudio AudioFLAC
        -- Video
        ".mp4" -> Just $ MediaVideo VideoMP4
        ".webm" -> Just $ MediaVideo VideoWebM
        ".mov" -> Just $ MediaVideo VideoMOV
        ".avi" -> Just $ MediaVideo VideoAVI
        -- Documents
        ".pdf" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppPDF
        ".json" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppJSON
        ".xml" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppXML
        ".zip" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppZip
        -- Text files (as octet-stream for generic handling)
        ".txt" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppOctetStream
        ".md" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppOctetStream
        ".csv" -> Just $ MediaApplication AppOctetStream
        -- Unknown
        _ -> Nothing

-- | Get file extension helper.
getFileExtension :: FilePath -> Text
getFileExtension = Text.toLower . Text.pack . takeExtension

-- | Convert MediaType to MIME type string.
mediaTypeToMime :: MediaType -> Text
mediaTypeToMime (MediaImage ImagePNG) = "image/png"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaImage ImageJPEG) = "image/jpeg"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaImage ImageGIF) = "image/gif"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaImage ImageWebP) = "image/webp"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaImage ImageSVG) = "image/svg+xml"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaAudio AudioMP3) = "audio/mp3"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaAudio AudioWAV) = "audio/wav"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaAudio AudioOGG) = "audio/ogg"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaAudio AudioAAC) = "audio/aac"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaAudio AudioFLAC) = "audio/flac"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaAudio AudioMPEG) = "audio/mpeg"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaVideo VideoMP4) = "video/mp4"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaVideo VideoWebM) = "video/webm"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaVideo VideoMOV) = "video/quicktime"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaVideo VideoAVI) = "video/avi"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaVideo VideoOGG) = "video/ogg"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaApplication AppPDF) = "application/pdf"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaApplication AppJSON) = "application/json"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaApplication AppXML) = "application/xml"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaApplication AppOctetStream) = "application/octet-stream"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaApplication AppZip) = "application/zip"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaText TextPlain) = "text/plain"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaText TextHTML) = "text/html"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaText TextCSS) = "text/css"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaText TextCSV) = "text/csv"
mediaTypeToMime (MediaText TextMarkdown) = "text/markdown"

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- File Attachment
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Maximum file size for attachment (50MB)
maxFileSize :: Int
maxFileSize = 50 * 1024 * 1024

{- | Execute file attachment.

This function reads a file from disk and returns it as a base64-encoded
attachment suitable for multi-modal LLM interactions.

Returns:
* 'Right AttachFileResult' on successful attachment
* 'Left QueryError' if capability not enabled, file not found, or file too large
-}
executeAttachFile ::
    Tracer IO Trace ->
    Toolbox ->
    FilePath ->
    IO (Either QueryError AttachFileResult)
executeAttachFile tracer toolbox filePath = do
    runTracer tracer (FileAttachRequestedTrace filePath)

    -- Check if capability is enabled
    if SystemToolAttachFile `notElem` toolboxCapabilities toolbox
        then do
            let err = CapabilityNotEnabledError "attach-file"
            runTracer tracer (FileAttachErrorTrace filePath "Capability not enabled")
            pure $ Left err
        else do
            -- Check if file exists
            fileExists <- doesFileExist filePath
            if not fileExists
                then do
                    let err = FileNotFoundError filePath
                    runTracer tracer (FileAttachErrorTrace filePath "File not found")
                    pure $ Left err
                else do
                    -- Read file
                    result <- try $ BS.readFile filePath
                    case result of
                        Left (e :: SomeException) -> do
                            let errMsg = Text.pack $ show e
                            runTracer tracer (FileAttachErrorTrace filePath errMsg)
                            pure $ Left $ SystemInfoError $ "Failed to read file: " <> errMsg
                        Right content -> do
                            let fileSize = BS.length content

                            -- Check file size
                            if fileSize > maxFileSize
                                then do
                                    runTracer tracer (FileAttachErrorTrace filePath "File too large")
                                    pure $ Left $ FileTooLargeError filePath maxFileSize
                                else do
                                    -- Detect media type
                                    case detectMediaType filePath of
                                        Nothing -> do
                                            let errMsg = "Unsupported file type: " <> getFileExtension filePath
                                            runTracer tracer (FileAttachErrorTrace filePath errMsg)
                                            pure $ Left $ UnsupportedFileTypeError filePath (getFileExtension filePath)
                                        Just mediaType -> do
                                            let mimeType = mediaTypeToMime mediaType
                                            let base64Data = Text.decodeUtf8 $ B64.encode content

                                            runTracer tracer (FileAttachSuccessTrace filePath mediaType fileSize)

                                            pure $
                                                Right $
                                                    AttachFileResult
                                                        { attachFilePath = filePath
                                                        , attachMimeType = mimeType
                                                        , attachBase64Data = base64Data
                                                        , attachFileSize = fileSize
                                                        }

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Query Execution
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | Execute a query for a specific capability.

This function:
1. Checks if the requested capability is enabled
2. Gathers the system information for that capability
3. Returns a 'QueryResult' with the data

Returns:
* 'Right QueryResult' on successful execution
* 'Left QueryError' if capability is not enabled or system info cannot be gathered

Note: For the 'attach-file' capability, use 'executeAttachFile' instead.
-}
executeQuery :: Tracer IO Trace -> Toolbox -> Text -> IO (Either QueryError QueryResult)
executeQuery tracer toolbox capabilityName = do
    runTracer tracer (SystemInfoRequestedTrace capabilityName)
    startTime <- getCurrentTime

    -- Find the capability
    case capabilityFromName capabilityName of
        Nothing -> do
            let err = "Unknown capability: " <> capabilityName
            runTracer tracer (SystemInfoErrorTrace capabilityName err)
            pure $ Left $ SystemInfoError err
        Just capability -> do
            -- Check if capability is enabled
            if capability `notElem` toolboxCapabilities toolbox
                then pure $ Left $ CapabilityNotEnabledError capabilityName
                else do
                    -- Gather the information
                    result <- getCapabilityInfoWithTimeout capability toolbox
                    endTime <- getCurrentTime
                    let execTime = diffUTCTime endTime startTime

                    case result of
                        Left err -> do
                            runTracer tracer (SystemInfoErrorTrace capabilityName err)
                            pure $ Left $ SystemInfoError err
                        Right (_, value) -> do
                            runTracer tracer (SystemInfoRetrievedTrace capabilityName execTime)
                            pure $
                                Right $
                                    QueryResult
                                        { resultCapability = capabilityName
                                        , resultData = value
                                        , resultExecutionTime = execTime
                                        }

-- | Convert a capability name text to the corresponding SystemToolCapability.
capabilityFromName :: Text -> Maybe SystemToolCapability
capabilityFromName name = case name of
    "date" -> Just SystemToolDate
    "operating-system" -> Just SystemToolOperatingSystem
    "env-vars" -> Just SystemToolEnvVars
    "running-user" -> Just SystemToolRunningUser
    "hostname" -> Just SystemToolHostname
    "working-directory" -> Just SystemToolWorkingDirectory
    "process-info" -> Just SystemToolProcessInfo
    "uptime" -> Just SystemToolUptime
    "attach-file" -> Just SystemToolAttachFile
    _ -> Nothing

-- | Get the name for a capability.
capabilityToName :: SystemToolCapability -> Text
capabilityToName SystemToolDate = "date"
capabilityToName SystemToolOperatingSystem = "operating-system"
capabilityToName SystemToolEnvVars = "env-vars"
capabilityToName SystemToolRunningUser = "running-user"
capabilityToName SystemToolHostname = "hostname"
capabilityToName SystemToolWorkingDirectory = "working-directory"
capabilityToName SystemToolProcessInfo = "process-info"
capabilityToName SystemToolUptime = "uptime"
capabilityToName SystemToolAttachFile = "attach-file"

-- | Default timeout for system info gathering (5 seconds).
defaultTimeoutSeconds :: Int
defaultTimeoutSeconds = 5

{- | Gather system information for a capability with a timeout.

Uses race to implement a timeout, preventing the toolbox from hanging
on slow or blocking system calls.
-}
getCapabilityInfoWithTimeout ::
    SystemToolCapability ->
    Toolbox ->
    IO (Either Text (Text, Aeson.Value))
getCapabilityInfoWithTimeout capability toolbox = do
    let timeoutMicros = defaultTimeoutSeconds * 1000000
    result <- race (threadDelay timeoutMicros) (getCapabilityInfoInternal capability toolbox)
    case result of
        Left () -> pure $ Left "Timeout while gathering system information"
        Right val -> pure val

{- | Internal function to gather system information.

This function handles each capability type and returns the appropriate
data as a JSON value.
-}
getCapabilityInfoInternal ::
    SystemToolCapability ->
    Toolbox ->
    IO (Either Text (Text, Aeson.Value))
getCapabilityInfoInternal capability toolbox = do
    result <- try $ do
        (name, value) <- case capability of
            SystemToolDate -> getDateInfo
            SystemToolOperatingSystem -> getOperatingSystemInfo
            SystemToolEnvVars -> getEnvVarsInfo (toolboxEnvVarFilter toolbox)
            SystemToolRunningUser -> getRunningUserInfo
            SystemToolHostname -> getHostnameInfo
            SystemToolWorkingDirectory -> getWorkingDirectoryInfo
            SystemToolProcessInfo -> getProcessInfo
            SystemToolUptime -> getUptimeInfo
            SystemToolAttachFile -> error "Use executeAttachFile for attach-file capability"
        pure (name, value)
    case result of
        Left (e :: SomeException) -> pure $ Left $ Text.pack $ show e
        Right val -> pure $ Right val

{- | Gather date/time information.

Returns:
* Current UTC time
* Current local time
* System timezone
-}
getDateInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getDateInfo = do
    utcTime <- Time.getCurrentTime
    tz <- Time.getCurrentTimeZone
    let localTime = Time.utcToLocalTime tz utcTime
    let tzName = Time.timeZoneName tz
    let tzOffset = Time.timeZoneOffsetString tz

    pure $
        ( "date"
        , Aeson.object
            [ "utc" .= Time.formatTime Time.defaultTimeLocale "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%QZ" utcTime
            , "local" .= Time.formatTime Time.defaultTimeLocale "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%Q" localTime
            , "timezone" .= tzName
            , "timezoneOffset" .= tzOffset
            ]
        )

{- | Gather operating system information.

Reads from /etc/os-release on Linux systems, and gathers kernel
version and architecture information.
-}
getOperatingSystemInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getOperatingSystemInfo = do
    -- Try to read /etc/os-release
    osRelease <- readOsRelease

    -- Get kernel version from uname
    kernelVersion <- safeReadProcess "uname" ["-r"] ""

    -- Get architecture from uname
    arch <- safeReadProcess "uname" ["-m"] ""

    pure $
        ( "operating-system"
        , Aeson.object $
            catMaybes
                [ fmap ("name" .=) (lookup "NAME" osRelease)
                , fmap ("version" .=) (lookup "VERSION" osRelease)
                , fmap ("id" .=) (lookup "ID" osRelease)
                , fmap ("versionId" .=) (lookup "VERSION_ID" osRelease)
                , fmap ("prettyName" .=) (lookup "PRETTY_NAME" osRelease)
                , fmap ("kernel" .=) (safeHead kernelVersion)
                , fmap ("architecture" .=) (safeHead arch)
                ]
        )
  where
    safeHead [] = Nothing
    safeHead (x : _) = Just (Text.strip x)

{- | Parse /etc/os-release file.

Returns a list of key-value pairs from the file.
-}
readOsRelease :: IO [(Text, Text)]
readOsRelease = do
    result <- tryReadFile "/etc/os-release"
    case result of
        Left _ -> do
            -- Try /usr/lib/os-release as fallback
            result2 <- tryReadFile "/usr/lib/os-release"
            case result2 of
                Left _ -> pure []
                Right content -> pure $ parseOsRelease content
        Right content -> pure $ parseOsRelease content
  where
    parseOsRelease :: ByteString -> [(Text, Text)]
    parseOsRelease content =
        let lines' = BS.lines content
            pairs = map parseLine lines'
         in catMaybes pairs

    parseLine :: ByteString -> Maybe (Text, Text)
    parseLine line
        | BS.null line = Nothing
        | BS.head line == '#' = Nothing
        | otherwise =
            case BS.break (== '=') line of
                (key, rest)
                    | BS.null rest -> Nothing
                    | otherwise ->
                        let value = BS.drop 1 rest
                            -- Remove quotes if present
                            value' =
                                if BS.length value >= 2 && BS.head value == '"' && BS.last value == '"'
                                    then BS.drop 1 (BS.init value)
                                    else value
                         in Just (Text.decodeUtf8 key, Text.decodeUtf8 value')

{- | Gather environment variable information.

If a filter pattern is provided, only variables whose names contain
the pattern are included (case-sensitive substring match).
-}
getEnvVarsInfo :: Maybe Text -> IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getEnvVarsInfo mFilter = do
    env <- getEnvironment
    let filtered = case mFilter of
            Nothing -> env
            Just pattern ->
                let pattern' = Text.unpack pattern
                 in filter (\(k, _) -> pattern' `isInfixOf` k) env

    -- Convert to Text for JSON
    let envMap =
            KeyMap.fromList $
                map (\(k, v) -> (AesonKey.fromText $ Text.pack k, String $ Text.pack v)) filtered

    pure ("env-vars", Object envMap)

{- | Gather running user information.

Returns username, UID, GID, home directory, and shell.
-}
getRunningUserInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getRunningUserInfo = do
    uid <- getEffectiveUserID
    gid <- getEffectiveGroupID

    -- Try to get user info from passwd database
    userInfo <- try $ getUserEntryForID uid

    let (username, home, shell) = case userInfo of
            Left (_ :: SomeException) -> (Text.pack $ show uid, "", "")
            Right entry ->
                ( Text.pack $ userName entry
                , Text.pack $ homeDirectory entry
                , Text.pack $ userShell entry
                )

    pure $
        ( "running-user"
        , Aeson.object
            [ "username" .= username
            , "uid" .= (fromIntegral uid :: Int)
            , "gid" .= (fromIntegral gid :: Int)
            , "homeDirectory" .= home
            , "shell" .= shell
            ]
        )

-- | Gather hostname information.
getHostnameInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getHostnameInfo = do
    hostname <- lookupEnv "HOSTNAME"
    hostname' <- case hostname of
        Just h -> pure $ Text.pack h
        Nothing -> do
            -- Try to read from /proc/sys/kernel/hostname
            result <- tryReadFile "/proc/sys/kernel/hostname"
            case result of
                Left _ -> do
                    -- Fallback to uname -n
                    result2 <- safeReadProcess "uname" ["-n"] ""
                    pure $ maybe "" id (safeHead result2)
                Right content -> pure $ Text.strip $ Text.decodeUtf8 content

    pure ("hostname", Aeson.object ["hostname" .= hostname'])
  where
    safeHead [] = Nothing
    safeHead (x : _) = Just (Text.strip x)

-- | Gather working directory information.
getWorkingDirectoryInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getWorkingDirectoryInfo = do
    cwd <- getCurrentDirectory
    pure ("working-directory", Aeson.object ["path" .= Text.pack cwd])

{- | Gather process information.

Returns process ID and parent process ID.
-}
getProcessInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getProcessInfo = do
    pid <- getProcessID
    ppid <- getParentProcessID

    -- Try to get process name from /proc/self/comm
    commResult <- tryReadFile "/proc/self/comm"
    let procName = case commResult of
            Left _ -> ""
            Right content -> Text.strip $ Text.decodeUtf8 content

    pure $
        ( "process-info"
        , Aeson.object
            [ "pid" .= (fromIntegral pid :: Int)
            , "ppid" .= (fromIntegral ppid :: Int)
            , "name" .= procName
            ]
        )

{- | Gather system uptime information.

Reads from /proc/uptime on Linux systems.
-}
getUptimeInfo :: IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getUptimeInfo = do
    result <- tryReadFile "/proc/uptime"
    case result of
        Left err ->
            pure ("uptime", Aeson.object ["error" .= Text.pack err])
        Right content -> do
            let text = Text.decodeUtf8 content
            let uptimeStr = Text.takeWhile (/= ' ') text
            let uptimeSec = readMaybe (Text.unpack uptimeStr) :: Maybe Double

            pure $
                ( "uptime"
                , Aeson.object $
                    catMaybes
                        [ Just $ "seconds" .= uptimeSec
                        , fmap (\s -> "formatted" .= formatUptime s) uptimeSec
                        ]
                )
  where
    readMaybe :: (Read a) => String -> Maybe a
    readMaybe s = case reads s of
        [(x, "")] -> Just x
        _ -> Nothing

    formatUptime :: Double -> Text
    formatUptime seconds =
        let days = floor (seconds / 86400) :: Int
            hours = floor ((seconds - fromIntegral days * 86400) / 3600) :: Int
            minutes = floor ((seconds - fromIntegral days * 86400 - fromIntegral hours * 3600) / 60) :: Int
            secs = floor (seconds - fromIntegral days * 86400 - fromIntegral hours * 3600 - fromIntegral minutes * 60) :: Int
         in Text.pack $
                (if days > 0 then show days ++ "d " else "")
                    ++ (if hours > 0 then show hours ++ "h " else "")
                    ++ (if minutes > 0 then show minutes ++ "m " else "")
                    ++ show secs
                    ++ "s"

{- | Get capability info (public version).

This is a convenience function that wraps the internal implementation
with a default toolbox context (no env var filtering).
-}
getCapabilityInfo :: SystemToolCapability -> IO (Text, Aeson.Value)
getCapabilityInfo capability = do
    let toolbox = Toolbox "" "" [capability] Nothing undefined
    result <- getCapabilityInfoWithTimeout capability toolbox
    case result of
        Left err -> pure (capabilityToName capability, String err)
        Right val -> pure val

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Utility Functions
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-- | Try to read a file, returning Left with error message on failure.
tryReadFile :: FilePath -> IO (Either String ByteString)
tryReadFile path = do
    result <- try $ BS.readFile path
    case result of
        Left e
            | isDoesNotExistError e -> pure $ Left $ "File not found: " ++ path
            | otherwise -> pure $ Left $ "Error reading " ++ path ++ ": " ++ show e
        Right content -> pure $ Right content

{- | Safely execute a process and return stdout lines.

Returns empty list on error.
-}
safeReadProcess :: String -> [String] -> String -> IO [Text]
safeReadProcess cmd args input = do
    result <- try $ Process.readProcess cmd args input
    case result of
        Left (_ :: SomeException) -> pure []
        Right output -> pure $ map Text.pack $ lines output

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Result Formatting
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

{- | Format query results as JSON.

Returns a ByteString containing a JSON object with:
* "capability": The capability name
* "data": The capability data
* "executionTime": Execution time in seconds

Example output:

> {
>   "capability": "date",
>   "data": {
>     "utc": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.123456Z",
>     "local": "2024-01-15T11:30:00.123456",
>     "timezone": "CET",
>     "timezoneOffset": "+0100"
>   },
>   "executionTime": 0.001
> }
-}
formatResults :: QueryResult -> ByteString
formatResults result =
    LByteString.toStrict $ Aeson.encode jsonObj
  where
    jsonObj =
        Aeson.object
            [ "capability" .= resultCapability result
            , "data" .= resultData result
            , "executionTime" .= formatExecutionTime (resultExecutionTime result)
            ]

-- | Format execution time as seconds with 3 decimal places.
formatExecutionTime :: NominalDiffTime -> Double
formatExecutionTime = realToFrac

263	-- OpenAPI Toolbox Configuration
264	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
265	
266	{- | Configuration for an OpenAPI toolbox.
267	
268	This describes an OpenAPI specification to load as a toolbox.
269	The toolbox will fetch the spec from 'openApiSpecUrl' and make
270	all operations available as tools to the agent.
271	
272	The spec URL can be either:
273	* An HTTP/HTTPS URL (e.g., "https://api.example.com/openapi.json")
274	* A file URL (e.g., "file:///path/to/openapi.json")
275	
276	Example configuration:
277	
278	@
279	{
280	  "tag": "OpenAPIServer",
281	  "contents": {
282	    "specUrl": "https://api.example.com/openapi.json",
283	    "baseUrl": "https://api.example.com",
284	    "headers": {"X-API-Version": "v1"},
285	    "token": "${API_TOKEN}",
286	    "filter": {"tag": "PathPrefix", "contents": "/api/v1"},
287	    "lifetime": "conversation",
288	    "activation": "always",
289	    "secrets": [
290	      {
291	        "source": {"tag": "EnvVar", "contents": "API_KEY"},
292	        "decoder": {"tag": "Clear", "contents": true},
293	        "serializer": {"tag": "Header", "contents": ["X-API-Key", null]}
294	      }
295	    ]
296	  }
297	}
298	@
299	
300	The 'secrets' field allows flexible secret management with support for
301	multiple sources (environment variables, files, commands), encodings
302	(Base64, hex), and serialization locations (headers, query string).
303	
304	The optional 'filter' field allows restricting which endpoints are
305	exposed as tools. See 'EndpointPredicate' for the available filter
306	predicates. If no filter is specified, all endpoints are included.
307	
308	The 'activation' field controls progressive disclosure (default: 'AlwaysActivated').
309	-}
310	data OpenAPIServerDescription
311	    = OpenAPIServerDescription
312	    { openApiSpecUrl :: Text
313	    -- ^ URL to fetch the OpenAPI specification from (can be http/https or file://)
314	    , openApiBaseUrl :: Text
315	    -- ^ Base URL for API calls (can be different from spec URL)
316	    , openApiHeaders :: Maybe (Map Text Text)
317	    -- ^ Optional static headers to include in all requests
318	    , openApiToken :: Maybe Text
319	    -- ^ Optional Bearer token for authentication (legacy, prefer using 'secrets')
320	    , openApiFilter :: Maybe EndpointPredicate
321	    -- ^ Optional filter to restrict which endpoints are exposed as tools
322	    , openApiSecrets :: Maybe [Secret]
323	    -- ^ Optional list of secrets to resolve and include in requests
324	    , openApiActivation :: Maybe Activation
325	    -- ^ Optional activation mode (default: AlwaysActivated)
326	    }
327	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
328	
329	{- | Custom JSON options for OpenAPIServerDescription to use camelCase field names
330	(matching the existing Agent JSON style)
331	-}
332	openApiServerOptions :: Aeson.Options
333	openApiServerOptions =
334	    Aeson.defaultOptions
335	        { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = dropPrefix "openApi"
336	        , Aeson.omitNothingFields = True
337	        }
338	  where
339	    dropPrefix prefix str
340	        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str
341	        | otherwise = str
342	
343	instance ToJSON OpenAPIServerDescription where
344	    toJSON = Aeson.genericToJSON openApiServerOptions
345	    toEncoding = Aeson.genericToEncoding openApiServerOptions
346	
347	instance FromJSON OpenAPIServerDescription where
348	    parseJSON = Aeson.genericParseJSON openApiServerOptions

350	{- | Reference to an OpenAPI server configuration stored on disk.
351	
352	This allows the entire OpenAPI server configuration to be stored in a file
353	rather than embedded directly in the agent configuration. The file should
354	contain a JSON object matching the 'OpenAPIServerDescription' structure.
355	
356	Example configuration:
357	
358	@
359	{
360	  "tag": "OpenAPIServerOnDisk",
361	  "contents": "/path/to/openapi-config.json"
362	}
363	@
364	
365	The referenced file should contain:
366	
367	@
368	{
369	  "specUrl": "https://api.example.com/openapi.json",
370	  "baseUrl": "https://api.example.com",
371	  "headers": {"X-API-Version": "v1"},
372	  "token": "${API_TOKEN}",
373	  "filter": {"tag": "PathPrefix", "contents": "/api/v1"},
374	  "lifetime": "conversation",
375	  "activation": "always",
376	  "secrets": []
377	}
378	@
379	-}
380	newtype OpenAPIServerOnDisk = OpenAPIServerOnDisk
381	    { openApiConfigPath :: FilePath
382	    -- ^ Path to the JSON file containing the OpenAPI server configuration
383	    }
384	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
385	
386	instance ToJSON OpenAPIServerOnDisk where
387	    toJSON (OpenAPIServerOnDisk path) = Aeson.toJSON path
388	
389	instance FromJSON OpenAPIServerOnDisk where
390	    parseJSON v = OpenAPIServerOnDisk <$> Aeson.parseJSON v
391	
392	{- | Wrapper type for JSON serialization with tag.
393	Similar to McpServerDescription, this allows extensible toolbox types.
394	-}
395	data OpenAPIToolboxDescription
396	    = OpenAPIServer OpenAPIServerDescription
397	    | OpenAPIServerOnDiskDescription OpenAPIServerOnDisk
398	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
399	
400	instance ToJSON OpenAPIToolboxDescription where
401	    toJSON (OpenAPIServer val) =
402	        Aeson.object
403	            [ "tag" .= ("OpenAPIServer" :: Text)
404	            , "contents" .= val
405	            ]
406	    toJSON (OpenAPIServerOnDiskDescription val) =
407	        Aeson.object
408	            [ "tag" .= ("OpenAPIServerOnDisk" :: Text)
409	            , "contents" .= val
410	            ]
411	
412	instance FromJSON OpenAPIToolboxDescription where
413	    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject "OpenAPIToolboxDescription" $ \v -> do
414	        tag <- v .: "tag"
415	        case (tag :: Text) of
416	            "OpenAPIServer" ->
417	                OpenAPIServer <$> v .: "contents"
418	            "OpenAPIServerOnDisk" ->
419	                OpenAPIServerOnDiskDescription <$> v .: "contents"
420	            _ -> fail "expecting 'OpenAPIServer' or 'OpenAPIServerOnDisk' tag"

880	{- | Wrapper type for builtin toolbox descriptions with tag-based JSON serialization.
881	
882	This is a tagged union type that allows extensible builtin toolbox types.
883	New builtin toolbox types can be added in the future without breaking
884	existing configurations.
885	
886	Example configuration:
887	
888	@
889	{
890	  "builtinToolboxes": [
891	    {"tag": "SqliteToolbox", "contents": {"name": "memory", "description": "a set of memories", "path": "/path/to/memories.sqlite", "access": "read-write"}},
892	    {"tag": "SqliteToolbox", "contents": {"name": "guidelines", "description": "a set of guidelines", "path": "/path/to/guidelines.sqlite", "access": "read-only"}},
893	    {"tag": "SystemToolbox", "contents": {"name": "system", "description": "System context", "capabilities": ["date", "hostname"], "envVarFilter": null}},
894	    {"tag": "DeveloperToolbox", "contents": {"name": "developer", "description": "Development tools", "capabilities": ["validate-tool", "scaffold-agent", "read-file-range", "write-file-range"]}},
895	    {"tag": "LuaToolbox", "contents": {"name": "lua", "description": "Lua orchestration", "maxMemoryMB": 256, "maxExecutionTimeSeconds": 300, "allowedTools": ["bash"], "allowedPaths": [], "allowedHosts": []}}
896	  ]
897	}
898	@
899	-}
900	data BuiltinToolboxDescription
901	    = SqliteToolbox SqliteToolboxDescription
902	    | SystemToolbox SystemToolboxDescription
903	    | DeveloperToolbox DeveloperToolboxDescription
904	    | LuaToolbox LuaToolboxDescription
905	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
906	
907	instance ToJSON BuiltinToolboxDescription where
908	    toJSON (SqliteToolbox val) =
909	        Aeson.object
910	            [ "tag" .= ("SqliteToolbox" :: Text)
911	            , "contents" .= val
912	            ]
913	    toJSON (SystemToolbox val) =
914	        Aeson.object
915	            [ "tag" .= ("SystemToolbox" :: Text)
916	            , "contents" .= val
917	            ]
918	    toJSON (DeveloperToolbox val) =
919	        Aeson.object
920	            [ "tag" .= ("DeveloperToolbox" :: Text)
921	            , "contents" .= val
922	            ]
923	    toJSON (LuaToolbox val) =
924	        Aeson.object
925	            [ "tag" .= ("LuaToolbox" :: Text)
926	            , "contents" .= val
927	            ]
928	
929	instance FromJSON BuiltinToolboxDescription where
930	    parseJSON = Aeson.withObject "BuiltinToolboxDescription" $ \v -> do
931	        tag <- v .: "tag"
932	        case (tag :: Text) of
933	            "SqliteToolbox" ->
934	                SqliteToolbox <$> v .: "contents"
935	            "SystemToolbox" ->
936	                SystemToolbox <$> v .: "contents"
937	            "DeveloperToolbox" ->
938	                DeveloperToolbox <$> v .: "contents"
939	            "LuaToolbox" ->
940	                LuaToolbox <$> v .: "contents"
941	            _ -> fail "expecting 'SqliteToolbox', 'SystemToolbox', 'LuaToolbox', or 'DeveloperToolbox' tag"
942	
943	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
944	-- Skills Toolbox Configuration
945	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
946	
947	{- | Configuration for skills toolboxes per agentskills.io specification.
948	
949	Skills provide procedural knowledge and executable capabilities via
950	progressive disclosure. The agent configuration specifies:
951	- skillSources: Where to load skills from (local directories, git repos)
952	- autoEnableSkills: Which skills to enable automatically at session start
953	
954	Example configuration:
955	
956	@
957	{
958	  "skillSources": [
959	    {"tag": "SkillDirectory", "contents": "./skills"},
960	    {"tag": "SkillGitRepo", "contents": {"url": "https://github.com/example/shared-skills.git"}}
961	  ],
962	  "autoEnableSkills": ["pdf-processing", "code-review"]
963	}
964	@
965	
966	Progressive disclosure:
967	- Initially, only metadata tools are visible (skill_describe_{name}, skill_enable_{name})
968	- After skill_enable_{name} is called, script tools become available
969	- Tool availability is computed as a pure function of Session history
970

769	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
770	-- Developer Toolbox Configuration
771	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
772	
773	{- | Enumeration of available developer tools capabilities.
774	
775	Each capability represents a tool that can help developers write
776	and validate agents and tools.
777	-}
778	data DeveloperToolCapability
779	    = -- | Validate a tool script
780	      DevToolValidateTool
781	    | -- | Generate agent scaffolding
782	      DevToolScaffoldAgent
783	    | -- | Generate tool scaffolding
784	      DevToolScaffoldTool
785	    | -- | Show bash-tools specification
786	      DevToolShowSpec
787	    | -- | Validate an agent JSON file
788	      DevToolValidateAgent
789	    | -- | Create a new agent configuration
790	      DevToolCreateAgent
791	    | -- | Create a new tool script
792	      DevToolCreateTool
793	    | -- | Read specific line ranges from a file
794	      DevToolReadFileRange
795	    | -- | Replace line ranges in a file with new content
796	      DevToolWriteFileRange
797	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
798	
799	-- | Serialize DeveloperToolCapability as kebab-case strings.
800	instance ToJSON DeveloperToolCapability where
801	    toJSON DevToolValidateTool = Aeson.String "validate-tool"
802	    toJSON DevToolScaffoldAgent = Aeson.String "scaffold-agent"
803	    toJSON DevToolScaffoldTool = Aeson.String "scaffold-tool"
804	    toJSON DevToolShowSpec = Aeson.String "show-spec"
805	    toJSON DevToolValidateAgent = Aeson.String "validate-agent"
806	    toJSON DevToolCreateAgent = Aeson.String "create-agent"
807	    toJSON DevToolCreateTool = Aeson.String "create-tool"
808	    toJSON DevToolReadFileRange = Aeson.String "read-file-range"
809	    toJSON DevToolWriteFileRange = Aeson.String "write-file-range"
810	
811	-- | Parse DeveloperToolCapability from kebab-case strings.
812	instance FromJSON DeveloperToolCapability where
813	    parseJSON = Aeson.withText "DeveloperToolCapability" $ \txt ->
814	        case txt of
815	            "validate-tool" -> return DevToolValidateTool
816	            "scaffold-agent" -> return DevToolScaffoldAgent
817	            "scaffold-tool" -> return DevToolScaffoldTool
818	            "show-spec" -> return DevToolShowSpec
819	            "validate-agent" -> return DevToolValidateAgent
820	            "create-agent" -> return DevToolCreateAgent
821	            "create-tool" -> return DevToolCreateTool
822	            "read-file-range" -> return DevToolReadFileRange
823	            "write-file-range" -> return DevToolWriteFileRange
824	            other -> fail $ "Invalid DeveloperToolCapability: " ++ Text.unpack other ++ ". Expected one of: validate-tool, scaffold-agent, scaffold-tool, show-spec, validate-agent, create-agent, create-tool, read-file-range, write-file-range."
825	
826	{- | Configuration for the developer toolbox.
827	
828	This describes which developer tools should be made available to an agent.
829	These tools help with writing and validating agents and tools.
830	
831	Example configuration:
832	
833	@
834	{
835	  "tag": "DeveloperToolbox",
836	  "contents": {
837	    "name": "developer",
838	    "description": "Tools for developing agents and tools",
839	    "capabilities": ["validate-tool", "scaffold-agent", "scaffold-tool", "read-file-range", "write-file-range"],
840	    "lifetime": "conversation",
841	    "activation": "always"
842	  }
843	}
844	@
845	
846	The 'activation' field controls progressive disclosure (default: 'AlwaysActivated').
847	-}
848	data DeveloperToolboxDescription
849	    = DeveloperToolboxDescription
850	    { developerToolboxName :: Text
851	    -- ^ Unique name for this toolbox instance (used as tool prefix)
852	    , developerToolboxDescription :: Text
853	    -- ^ Human-readable description of the toolbox purpose
854	    , developerToolboxCapabilities :: [DeveloperToolCapability]
855	    -- ^ List of developer tool capabilities to expose
856	    , developerToolboxActivation :: Maybe Activation
857	    -- ^ Optional activation mode (default: AlwaysActivated)
858	    }
859	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
860	
861	-- | Custom JSON options for DeveloperToolboxDescription to use camelCase field names
862	developerToolboxOptions :: Aeson.Options
863	developerToolboxOptions =
864	    Aeson.defaultOptions
865	        { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = dropPrefix "developerToolbox"
866	        , Aeson.omitNothingFields = True
867	        }
868	  where
869	    dropPrefix prefix str
870	        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str

599	-- Builtin Toolbox Configuration
600	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
601	
602	{- | Access mode for SQLite databases.
603	
604	Controls whether the database is opened in read-only, read-write, or snapshot mode.
605	This affects both file permissions and SQLite's internal locking behavior.
606	
607	* 'SqliteReadOnly': Open database in read-only mode. No modifications allowed.
608	* 'SqliteReadWrite': Open database in read-write mode. Both reads and writes allowed.
609	* 'SqliteSnapshot': Create a copy of the database for this conversation and open it
610	  in read-write mode. Changes are isolated to the conversation lifetime.
611	-}
612	data SqliteAccessMode
613	    = -- | Open database in read-only mode. No modifications allowed.
614	      SqliteReadOnly
615	    | -- | Open database in read-write mode. Both reads and writes allowed.
616	      SqliteReadWrite
617	    | {- | Create a snapshot copy for this conversation, opened in read-write mode.
618	      The snapshot is created on first use using the conversation ID as suffix.
619	      -}
620	      SqliteSnapshot
621	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
622	
623	-- | Serialize SqliteAccessMode as kebab-case strings.
624	instance ToJSON SqliteAccessMode where
625	    toJSON SqliteReadOnly = Aeson.String "read-only"
626	    toJSON SqliteReadWrite = Aeson.String "read-write"
627	    toJSON SqliteSnapshot = Aeson.String "snapshot"
628	
629	-- | Parse SqliteAccessMode from kebab-case strings.
630	instance FromJSON SqliteAccessMode where
631	    parseJSON = Aeson.withText "SqliteAccessMode" $ \txt ->
632	        case txt of
633	            "read-only" -> return SqliteReadOnly
634	            "read-write" -> return SqliteReadWrite
635	            "snapshot" -> return SqliteSnapshot
636	            other -> fail $ "Invalid SqliteAccessMode: " ++ Text.unpack other ++ ". Expected 'read-only', 'read-write', or 'snapshot'."
637	
638	{- | Configuration for a SQLite builtin toolbox.
639	
640	This describes a SQLite database to load as a builtin toolbox.
641	The toolbox provides tools for querying and optionally modifying
642	the SQLite database.
643	
644	Example configuration:
645	
646	@
647	{
648	  "name": "memory",
649	  "description": "a set of memories",
650	  "path": "/path/to/memories.sqlite",
651	  "access": "read-write",
652	  "lifetime": "conversation",
653	  "activation": "always"
654	}
655	@
656	
657	The 'access' field controls whether the database is opened in
658	read-only, read-write, or snapshot mode. Use 'read-only' for safety when
659	the agent should only query data, 'read-write' when the agent
660	needs to modify the database directly, and 'snapshot' when you want

1	{-# LANGUAGE DeriveGeneric #-}
2	{-# LANGUAGE GeneralizedNewtypeDeriving #-}
3	{-# LANGUAGE LambdaCase #-}
4	{-# LANGUAGE OverloadedRecordDot #-}
5	
6	module System.Agents.Base where
7	
8	import Data.Aeson (FromJSON (..), ToJSON (..), (.:), (.=))
9	import qualified Data.Aeson as Aeson
10	import Data.Char (toLower)
11	import Data.Map.Strict (Map)
12	import Data.Text (Text)
13	import qualified Data.Text as Text
14	import Data.UUID (UUID)
15	import qualified Data.UUID.V4 as UUID
16	import GHC.Generics (Generic)
17	
18	import System.Agents.Tools.Activation (Activation)
19	import System.Agents.Tools.EndpointPredicate (EndpointPredicate)
20	import System.Agents.Tools.PostgREST.Types (HttpMethod (..))
21	import System.Agents.Tools.Secrets (Secret)
22	import System.Agents.Tools.Skills.Types (SkillName, SkillSource)
23	
24	type AgentSlug = Text
25	type AgentAnnounce = Text
26	
27	newtype AgentId = AgentId UUID
28	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, FromJSON, ToJSON)
29	
30	newAgentId :: IO AgentId
31	newAgentId =
32	    AgentId <$> UUID.nextRandom
33	
34	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	newtype ConversationId = ConversationId UUID
36	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, FromJSON, ToJSON)
37	
38	newConversationId :: IO ConversationId
39	newConversationId =
40	    ConversationId <$> UUID.nextRandom
41	
42	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	newtype StepId = StepId UUID
44	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, FromJSON, ToJSON)
45	
46	newStepId :: IO StepId
47	newStepId =
48	    StepId <$> UUID.nextRandom
49	
50	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	-- Note: SessionId and TurnId are defined in System.Agents.Session.Types
52	-- to avoid circular dependencies between Session.Base and Tools.Context.
53	-- They are re-exported from Session.Base for convenience.
54	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	{- | Reference to an additional agent outside the toolDirectory hierarchy
57	
58	This enables agents to reference other agents explicitly, supporting
59	self-references and mutual recursion in the agent tree.
60	-}
61	data ExtraAgentRef
62	    = ExtraAgentRef
63	    { extraAgentSlug :: AgentSlug
64	    -- ^ The slug to use when referring to this agent
65	    , extraAgentPath :: FilePath
66	    -- ^ Path to the agent's JSON configuration file
67	    }
68	    deriving (Show, Ord, Eq, Generic)
69	
70	-- | Custom JSON options for ExtraAgentRef to use hyphenated field names
71	extraAgentRefOptions :: Aeson.Options
72	extraAgentRefOptions =
73	    Aeson.defaultOptions
74	        { Aeson.fieldLabelModifier = kebabCase . dropPrefix "extraAgent"
75	        }
76	  where
77	    -- Convert camelCase to kebab-case
78	    kebabCase [] = []
79	    kebabCase (c : cs) = toLower c : go cs
80	      where
81	        go [] = []
82	        go (x : xs)
83	            | x `elem` ['A' .. 'Z'] = '-' : toLower x : go xs
84	            | otherwise = x : go xs
85	    -- Drop the "extraAgent" prefix from field names
86	    dropPrefix prefix str
87	        | take (length prefix) str == prefix = drop (length prefix) str
88	        | otherwise = str
89	
90	instance ToJSON ExtraAgentRef where
91	    toJSON = Aeson.genericToJSON extraAgentRefOptions
92	    toEncoding = Aeson.genericToEncoding extraAgentRefOptions
93	
94	instance FromJSON ExtraAgentRef where
95	    parseJSON = Aeson.genericParseJSON extraAgentRefOptions
96
